transcript: Lucas Dostal getting the start in net tonight for Anaheim and it's Arvid Soderblom at the other end for the Hawks long point shot off of Dostal Ryan Donato trying to get it through the neutral zone for the Hawks turned away and Silverberg and Ross Johnston into the Chicago zone Johnston centers off the stick of Soderblom F Fowler streaking up that left wing. Just couldn't get him to pocket it. Here comes Bedard. Long wrist shot is in the glove of Lucas Dostal. But that one bounced away from Max Jones. Gleason recovers in the neutral zone. Storms back in and fires. And Soderblom makes the stop. Johnston trying to backhand it up the boards, but that was sniffed out by Glassick. Joey Anderson finds Seth Jones, and Dostal hangs on to his shot. Behind the net, Lacombe trying to center, and it's tracked down by Strom, who skated right through the goal crease. Back for Mentyakov. Lacombe one-timer blockered away by Sodderbloom. I like to see the one-timer from from Lacombe there. Blocked out of the zone by Leeson. It's a foot race to the puck in the Chicago end. Leeson wins it. Leeson looks at Max Jones, looks him off, and now drops it back to the line. Fowler through traffic, and that's steered to the corner. Taylor Radish for the Hawks will feed it back to the line. Kevin Korchinski, former first-round draft pick, and that pass was redirected to the front of the net, trying to find Bedard. Hawks break out. Reichel with a nice little piece of stick handling, and here's Bedard. One-on-one with Lindstrom. Tries to make a pass, and it ricocheted off the Duck defender. Made his way to San Diego, and now made his way onto the Anaheim Ducks organizational roster. Here's Seth Jones trying to skate right in on Sucanek's good buddy, Lucas Dostal. And it popped over the net behind. Bedard tries to wrap it around again. And it's covered. Seth Jones really jumping on his horse. And a little bit of confusion. You see two players a little bit uncertain about who was going to get the angle on Seth Jones. And so the big D just said, you know what, I'm going to keep on going. There are probably six to ten media members waiting for Bedard. And Tavish finds Petrano in front and Soderblom turns him away. Let's have a look at it. Good saves, series of saves by Soderblom and there's the cross check. He and Myers at center, and Myers got it only as far as the strike. Ryan Donato turns back the other way. Seventh-year man out of Harvard. Got it on net, and a good save by Dostal. Radish got to the rebound. Back to the line set. Jones leans into it, deflected, and fought off by Dostal. Cut off by Gus Lindstrom. Good play there, and he'll angle it center ice. Hooked into the middle for Vetrano by McTavish. His long wrist shot gloved off by Soderblom. Watch a lot of games. Jones coughs it up. Here's Terry, two-on-one. He'll take the shot and way out with Soderblom to kick it aside. Here's the play where Troy Terry pokes it away from Seth Jones. A clear cut two on one. Pulls it into his wheelhouse. Look where the goaltender is perfectly positioned. Strome does the same but Slaggart off a skate brings it right back in. Landon Slaggart takes the shot. Stick save. Dostal comes right back to the side of the net. You're feeling the pressure to produce. The team's having a tough time scoring goals. You can understand why you go there. Gus Lindstrom with a shot that's tipped in. And Anaheim strikes first. I think it's Killorn who got a piece of this. Simple play, right? They go low to high. Change the angle of attack. Pass it across the blue line. All the result of a face-off win. And and the Ducks have been doing a good job and it's Carlson that wins it back there's the shot that pucks going about a foot wide and Alice cologne looked like he was gonna be all tied up but he reached out a little touch pulled it back against the grain and just over the glove of the goaltender. Killoun steps in to take the draw silverberg sweeps it to foul it across the top strong pulls it in just missed all the way to the goal line strong gives it back fowler's shot through traffic the last college u.s college player to score that many goals in a season was eight years ago that's kyle conowler's shot through traffic. The last college U.S. college player to score that many goals in a season was eight years ago. That's Kyle Conner who's done pretty good scoring goals at the National Hockey League level. Now of the Winnipeg Jets. Across the top. Zellweger gets it back from Killorn. Into the slot. Nice pass. Carlson's shot fought off by Soderblom. Dickinson stood up by Mityakov at the blue line and Anaheim gets possession back. Nice job by Pavel We'll be possession back. Nice job by Pavel there. Leeson into the Chicago zone. He scores! Used an advancement as a screen, pulls it in. It's 2-0. Well, that was a good rip by Brett Leeson, and, you know, he has surprised a number of goaltenders this year. So the shooting angle is a little bit different. If the goaltender's not set up on the puck and he's set up on the body. Boy, that was beautiful. He pulled the string on that. Watch him pull that puck into his wheelhouse and just snap it right through the legs of the defenseman. Establish a new career high for games played. Here's a loose puck, and Leeson again got to it in the Chicago zone, but a diving effort by Magnum knocked it away. It was the right idea. Unfortunately, that puck just bounced right over a stick. Myers and Minyakoff pick up the assist on the goal moments ago by Leeson, and here's a good save by Dostalostal again the Ducks sloppy with the puck in their own zone Taylor Radish had a shorthanded chance now Strom enters the zone trying to find Vetrano who keeps the puck alive and now out high it's Fowler into the bump position, Killorn gives it back to Fowler, Vetrano's one-timer handled by Soderblom up the wall, pinching his Kaiser, the defenseman this point covered by Reichel across to Tenorti, his shot gobbled up Thank you. Handled by Soderblom. Up the wall, pinching his Kaiser, the defenseman. This point covered by Reichel. Across to Tenorti. His shot gobbled up by Lucas Dostal. Seth Jones trying to protect back in the Chicago zone. That was a good job by Carlson. He was pressuring Vlasic at the blue line, and Troy Terry sniffed that out. Here's Bedard, and that went high off of, I believe, the mask of Dostal, and Carlson is hurt. Oh boy, it's his knee, John. All alone in front, great save on Kurashev. That's third man advantage of the game. They're 0-2, but as you point out, it's the rare 4-on-3 for the first 56 seconds. Fowler takes a wrist shot. Cam, high slot, redirect from McTavish, good save, Soderboom, Terry he missed, a gaping net McTavish across the top gets it back from Zellweger fires it off the skate of Radish penalty is over, Bedard out of the box Ducks still working the Chicago zone, the open man is Killorn and he scores second of the game for Alex Killorn. Good things happen when you shoot the puck. I'm going to be showing a lot of video about good things. Shooting the puck from everywhere. Killorn just pulls that into his wheelhouse. Soto blooms down. Watch the goaltender as he gets back up to his feet. I always talk about moving into the puck. You see him reaching with the right leg. Terry, cross ice. Vetrano missed on the short side. Chased around the boards by Terry, but it's Fowler with the keep. Half a minute of the two-man advantage. Vetrano has time. He scores! Number 30 for Frank! Boy, he's been so close for so long. And you know the Ducks have been looking for him. Pass from Cam Fowler. He's allowed to step in and just puts it right up and under the bar it's a great shot from frank he had time to really measure the goaltender and that's bar down the reaction says it all he had gone nine games without a goal before tonight fans come to their feet and Lucas Dostal has his first career NHL shutout the first of the year for the Ducks leaving only the New Jersey Devils as the only team in the NHL who don't have a whitewash the Ducks get the win for nothing
